item.title: All Confirmed & Rumored Games For Sony State Of Play 2026
item.content:encoded:
The next Sony State of Play is scheduled for June 2, and it’s being hyped up as one of the biggest yet. Not only are Sony hiring out cinemas for people to watch the show live, but the announcement for the showcase was made two weeks in advance, with a timer ticking down to the event.

It’s got fans speculating that this could be a heavy-hitting show, and for good reason, it seems! Still, it begs the question: what games are going to feature during the June 2026 Sony State of Play?

To help you prepare for the big event, we’ve collected a list of every confirmed and rumored game for the State of Play—check them all out below.

Every Confirmed Game For June 2026 State Of Play

So far, only one game has been confirmed for the upcoming State of Play, and that’s Insomniac’s Wolverine.

It was revealed alongside the announcement for the showcase that Wolverine would open the show with an in-depth look at combat and gameplay. Expect to see extended sequences and visceral, gory combat, though it shouldn’t stop there.

  • More Insider Gaming: Official Summer Game Fest 2026 Event Calendar

Insomniac have been clear that pre-orders wouldn’t open until fans saw more of the game. Well, with the State of Play undoubtedly doing so, it’s reasonable to assume that pre-orders should open once the reveal is over—who knows, we might even get information on a deluxe edition.

Every Rumored Game For June 2026 State Of Play

There are plenty of games that are rumored for the State of Play. With a runtime of over 60 minutes, there should be plenty of reveals and surprises in store, but here’s what we think has a strong chance of appearing based on rumors and speculation.

God Of War Faye Game/Original Trilogy Remake

It’s long been rumored that a Cory Barlog-produced God of War spinoff was in development at Santa Monica, and it would feature Kratos’ wife Faye as the lead. According to numerous insiders, gameplay will differ from the Norse games, with more of a focus on action, akin to Devil May Cry. Could this be the closing announcement to end the showcase with a bang?

As if that wasn’t enough, the Original Trilogy Remake was announced during February’s State of Play, and there’s every chance we’ll see more of that project. There’s God of War galore, and it’s fair to assume at least one of these projects appears.

Intergalactic: The Heretic Prophet

Naughty Dog has long been a pillar of PlayStation, and aside from the countless Last of Us remakes, fans are desperate to see more of its new IP, Intergalactic: The Heretic Prophet.

Other than its announcement trailer from the 2024 Game Awards, little else has been said regarding the title. With an enthusiastic cast raving about the game, it’s high time we heard more about what Naughty Dog have been so hard at work on, even if it’s still a ways away.

Final Fantasy 7 Part 3

The Final Fantasy 7 remakes have been an incredible showcase for how remakes can drastically alter familiar stories and build upon them in new and exciting ways. With the final entry into the trilogy rounding out the 1997 JRPG classic, there’s a high chance we see an official reveal during the State of Play—even though Part 3 won’t be PlayStation exclusive.

Kena: Scars Of Kosmora

Another game that was announced during the February 2026 State of Play, Kena: Scars of Kosmora is the sequal to Kena: Bridge of Spirits. The developers mentioned that their partnership with PlayStation has allowed them to expand the Kena world while staying true to the original.

  • More Insider Gaming: Best Sony PlayStation Days of Play 2026 Deals: End Date & Sale Games

It’s looking like a real step-up, and with a release window of this year, it’d be a surprise if it didn’t show up during the upcoming showcase.

New Gran Turismo

It’s been a while since we last had a Gran Turismo title, with Gran Turismo 7 releasing in 2022. Well, it’s been confirmed that a new entry into the franchise is in development, and if it were to be shown anywhere, it’s likely to be during a State of Play. The question is simply whether it’s ready to be revealed, but time will surely tell.

Phantom Blade Zero

With a September 2026 release date, Phantom Blade Zero seems like a given to appear at the State of Play. The action-RPG has a lot of hype surrounding it, and with Wolverine also releasing in September, it’s looking like a packed month for Sony.

New Media Molecule Title

It’s been a long time since we’ve heard from Media Molecule, with its last release being Dreams back in 2020, but it didn’t exactly set the world alight, with it being more of a game-creation tool.

Well, based on job descriptions, Media Molecule’s new title will feature open-world content, with a new IP supposedly attached. I, for one, am always hoping for the return of LittleBigPlanet, but anything from the Media Molecule team would make me a happy chap.

Fairgame$ Gets A Rebrand

Insider Gaming has long reported on Fairgame$, with internal playtests supposedly not quite hitting the mark. Well, a few weeks before the State of Play, Sony trademarked a new game called Break In, and it’s rumored to be a rebranding for Fairgame$. Whether this will help improve the game’s chances remains to be seen, but we could soon find out during this State of Play.

Other Potential Appearances

There’s plenty more out there that could appear during the State of Play, and there’s bound to be a few surprises in store that managed to avoid the leak plague. That said, here are a few more games that could also receive a new announcement:

  • Resident Evil 9 DLC
  • Resident Evil Code Veronica Remake
  • Rayman Legends Retold
  • Marvel Tokon: Fighting Souls New Characters
  • Silent Hill Townfall
  • Onimusha Way of the Sword
  • Forza Horizon 6 PS5 Release Date
  • 4Loop
  • Horizon Hunters Gathering
  • Castlevania Belmont’s Curse

What are you hoping to see during the State of Play? Share your predictions in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming content, check out the following:

  • Insider Gaming Showcase—Everything Announced
  • Xbox Games Showcase 2026 & Gears of War: E-Day Direct—Date, Start Times & How to Watch
  • Summer Game Fest 2026 Showcase—Date & Start Times, How to Watch & How Long it is

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

item.title: Magical Craft: My Enchanted Dress Shop coming west on October 2
item.content:encoded:
Publisher Imagineer and developer syn Sophia will release Magical Craft: My Enchanted Dress Shop for Switch both physically and digitally on October 2 n the west, the company announced. It will support English, French, German, Japanese, Korean, Traditional Chinese, and Simplified Chinese language options.

Before the western release, a digital-only Asian release with English, Traditional Chinese, and Simplified Chinese language support will launch on June 18.

Magical Craft: My Enchanted Dress Shop first launched for Switch on November 6, 2025 in Japan.

Here is an overview of the game, via its official website:

About

Magical Craft: My Enchanted Dress Shop is a life simulation game centered on dressmaking and dress-up, set in the beautiful village of Fleune, surrounded by picturesque streets and nature. Players take on the role of Lilia, a dressmaker who responds to requests from the villagers while working toward becoming a “legendary dressmaker” through the creation of a wide variety of dresses.

Enjoy a relaxing and heartwarming experience in a gentle watercolor-style world, where time flows at a leisurely pace.

Story

Blue skies, southern breezes, and cats—the tale of a little dressmaker.

Welcome to “Fleune”, a beautiful village with lovely streets and lush, green nature.

Here, people use a gentle power called “Practical Magic” to make every day a little more special.

Among these powers is “Sewing Magic.” Lilia grew up watching her grandmother use it to create wonderful dresses that bring joy to everyone. Inspired by her, Lilia decides she wants to make people happy too.

Now, she is taking her first step toward becoming a legendary dressmaker.

Introduction

Create. Wear. Share.

Bringing happiness to everyone through dressmaking.

Stroll and Explore

Take a stroll through the village to enjoy shopping, gather materials for your dresses, and chat with the villagers.

  • The beautiful scenery of Fleune Village—like a page from a storybook come to life.
  • Discover dress materials while enjoying a peaceful stroll.
  • Every item you find can become a beautiful material for your next dress.
  • Your faithful companion, Jam—the cat who goes everywhere with you.
  • The village is full of adorable cats! Befriend them to receive special gifts.

Taking Requests

Listen to the villagers’ wishes and design their perfect dresses.

  • Craft dresses to fulfill requests from the villagers!
  • Villagers will react differently depending on the dress you wear.
  • Completing requests even lets you coordinate outfits for the villagers!
  • Fill the entire village with your own signature style.

Creating Your Own Dresses

Craft one-of-a-kind dream dresses that are uniquely yours. As you level up as a dressmaker, you’ll unlock more designs to create and new places to explore!

  • Mix and match patterns, fabrics, and parts to create your perfect dress.
  • Over 100,000 original fabric combinations by layering colors, patterns, and motifs!
  • Once your design is ready, use “Sewing Magic” for the finishing touch.
  • Show off your latest creations as you stroll through town!
  • Level up to discover even more beautiful locations to visit.

Characters

  • Lilia – A girl who dreams of becoming a legendary dressmaker. She lives with her grandmother, who runs a local tailor shop. After her grandmother falls ill, Lilia awakens to her own magical talent. By collaborating with the villagers and gathering rare materials, she creates one-of-a-kind, adorable dresses for the world.
  • Sauge – Lilia’s grandmother and a former world-class dressmaker. She guides the protagonist throughout the story with her expert advice. Her favorite hobby is baking delicious sweets.
  • Lyra – Like the protagonist, Lyra is a young girl striving to become a legendary dressmaker. As a skilled dyer, she uses “Dyeing Magic” to create fabrics with breathtakingly beautiful patterns.
  • Neme – A magical artist living in a quiet cottage. If you bring the right key items, Neme will paint exquisite designs and motifs onto your canvas fabrics.
  • Alissa – A sophisticated older-sister figure who works at the local café. She has a calm and mature personality, yet she loves to wear outfits with cute, voluminous frills. She is actually Neme’s younger sister.
  • Zip – The friendly young man who runs the variety store. He’s always looking out for the protagonist as she pursues her dream. With his kind heart and helpful nature, he’s the big brother figure everyone in the village loves.
  • Anne – The chic owner of the village’s only boutique. She sells essential tools for sewing magic alongside a stylish collection of shoes. Anne always welcomes her customers with a gentle and serene smile.

Watch the announcement trailer below.

Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/magical-craft-my-enchanted-dress-shop-coming-west-on-october-2

item.title: Studio Founded By Forza Horizon 5 Director Announces Open-World Action-Driving Game Clutch
item.content:encoded:
Maverick Games has announced an open-world action-driving game called Clutch. Clutch will be the debut game for the studio, which was founded by Mike Brown, who designed Forza Horizon 2, 3, and 4, and served as creative director for Forza Horizon 5. While that history with perhaps the premier open-world racing franchise will help in creating Clutch, it sounds as though this new title will be more cinematic and narrative-driven.

Clutch features a cinematic, story-driven campaign telling the story of sibling racers competing in a historic racing series called the R1K. Within the R1K, players can take part in sanctioned events. However, on the other side of the campaign is the Midnight Collective, the underground circuit that's more about style. The hero of the story finds himself in trouble, and the seedier elements of the world are brought to light.

As you play, you jockey for position in races, while earning reputation and rewards within the broader narrative. According to a press release, players will need to react to and navigate shifting alliances and rival crews within the context of a living PvPvE world. Missions are hand-crafted, with both planned and spontaneous missions popping up as you speed through the game. Maverick Games touts its proprietary physics engine and open-world sandbox as key factors in delivering cinematic action and emergent, chaotic opportunities within the game.

In addition to the racing and story, players can expect deep vehicle customization, which includes the standard of custom paint and aftermarket parts, but also other elements that Maverick Games claims will help players create a one-of-a-kind ride and deliver on the tiny details that, "build on the connection between car and driver." 

While this is Maverick Games' first title, Brown's work on the Forza Horizon series bolsters confidence. The Forza Horizon franchise has always scored well, with 5 serving as the pinnacle in the eyes of many. That game, of which Brown was creative director, earned a 9.5 out of 10 from Game Informer when it launched in 2021.

We will learn more about Clutch during the Summer Game Fest Showcase this Friday, June 5. Until then, if this sounds up your alley, you can look forward to playing Clutch when it launches spring 2027.



https://gameinformer.com/2026/06/02/studio-founded-by-forza-horizon-5-director-announces-open-world-action-driving-game
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Streets of Rage movie gains momentum as Sonic the Hedgehog writers and The Harder They Fall director board the adaptation
item.content:encoded:
Though you may have forgotten about it, a Streets of Rage movie has been in the works since 2022. Originally, Hollywood's current go-to action screenwriter Derek Kolstad (of John Wick fame) was attached. Now, the slow-moving project has evolved and recruited two new writers, plus a director.

Via Deadline, we've learned the live-action movie project is "alive and well at Lionsgate" with Sonic the Hedgehog writers Pat Casey and Josh Miller, who have been attached to all four movies (there's another one coming next year) based on Sega's mascot so far. Considering Sega is behind both franchises, the creative choice feels logical.

But I think the news on who's going to be behind the camera is more exciting: The Harder They Fall filmmaker Jeymes Samuel has boarded the project. Since a heavy rewrite is now expected, it'll be a while before we even get a tentative release window.

Plot details are being kept under wraps, but Streets of Rage's big-screen debut was described in past press releases as "leaning heavily into the cult classic game known for its iconic characters, acclaimed electronic soundtracks and beat 'em up action". Lionsgate has, however, shared some words about Samuel's hiring: "Jeymes Samuel's visceral and explosive style - not to mention his signature use of unforgettable music - is perfect for audiences both familiar with and new to the series. We are so excited for how he will bring his own memorable take to this beloved video game IP," said Lionsgate film chief Erin Westerman.

Back in 2020, Dotemu, Guard Crush, and Lizardcube's Streets of Rage 4 greatly reinvigorated the beat 'em up series, and Streets of Rage: Revolution is currently in development. Eurogamer's five-star review of Streets of Rage 4 called it "a sequel that manages to best its forebears".

Mentioned in this article



https://www.eurogamer.net/streets-of-rage-movie-sonic-writers

item.title: PlayStation Reveals FlexStrike Fight Stick Release Date
item.content:encoded:
PlayStation has stated that the FlexStrike Fight Stick will release in August 2026.

The new fight stick was announced back in July 2025 for PS5 and PCs, with the blog post at the time offering players a glimpse of the features of the new fighting-games-inspired wireless controller. The latest announcement not only provides the release date but also the price, pre-order details, and availability for the FlexStrike.

PlayStation FlexStrike Fight Stick Release Date Set for August 6

A new blog post by PlayStation reveals that the FlexStrike Fight Stick will release alongside MARVEL Tōkon: Fighting Souls on August 6, 2026. The purchase will include a sling carry case and a built-in rechargeable battery. Interested buyers can pre-order the controller starting Jun 12, 2026, at $199.99/€199.99/£179.99/¥34,980 (including tax). It will be available through direct.playstation.com and participating retailers.

Along with the new fighting controller, PlayStation is releasing a 27-inch Gaming Monitor with DualSense Charging Hook in the United States and Japan on August 27, 2026. It has been priced at $349.99/¥49,980, which comes with a 27-inch QHD IPS display (2560×1440) with VRR, supporting up to 120Hz on PS5 and PS5 Pro, and up to 240Hz on a compatible PC or Mac. Additionally, the announcement also revealed that a set of Pulse Elevate wireless speakers will arrive later this year.

In other news, PlayStation saw a massive 50% drop in first-party sales between 2020 and 2024, prompting many to wonder how the firm recovers from here. Additionally, PlayStation has caught the neck of a major shovelware publisher, leading to the delisting of over 1,000 games.

Will you be purchasing the PlayStation FlexStrike fight stick once it releases this August? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about a new Sly Cooper game being hinted at. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: All Leaked LEGO x Pokémon Sets: Set Sizes, Smart Bricks & Expected Prices
item.content:encoded:
LEGO’s huge collab with the Pokémon franchise is set to get even better, with leaks showing a wide range of products and sets on the horizon.

Initially announced last year, the first LEGO Pokémon sets were released in February, but the next drop is set to dwarf the initial launch significantly with three times as many products coming to market.

For all the details on the upcoming LEGO Pokémon sets, Insider Gaming has you covered.

All Leaked Upcoming LEGO Pokémon Sets

15 new LEGO Pokémon sets have been leaked ahead of their official reveal, 12 of which already have images available, in a drop that is set to be significantly larger than the drop in February.

While February’s drop, which debuted LEGO Pokémon sets, was geared towards adult builders, the next range is predominantly geared towards children, with 11 of the leaked sets featuring Smart Brick technology.

  • More Insider Gaming: All Pokémon 30th Anniversary Celebration TCG Classic Cards Have Already Leaked

There will be something for everyone, however, with four other larger sets, three of which contain over 1,000 pieces, while the smallest set has over 750 pieces, still larger than all but two of the Smart Brick sets.

Check out the table below for the set names, set numbers, number of pieces, Smart Brick details, and expected price ranges based on similar products. Price ranges are based on leaks or comparable products available on the LEGO store.

When Are New LEGO Pokémon Sets Being Released?

The next range of LEGO Pokémon sets is expected to be released in August, with many have a reported drop date of August 1.

Like the majority of LEGO sets, pre-orders will be available ahead of time, and unlike the fierce frenzy that there is to secure TCG products, obtaining the LEGO Pokémon sets should be much easier.

  • More Insider Gaming: Pokémon TCG 30th Celebration Complete Set List—All Revealed Cards & Teasers

The three main LEGO Pokémon sets that were released in February are still available to buy at MSRP, either directly from the LEGO Store or other retailers, while the Badge Set and Mini Pokémon Center are no longer available, as they were only distributed for a limited time with qualifying purchases, or VIP Points.

It remains to be seen whether there will be more exclusive sets in this drop of LEGO Pokémon products, but full details should be available soon.

What do you think about the next range of LEGO Pokémon products? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Pokémon, check out the winning team of the first Pokémon Champions VGC Regional, and read about the global sales numbers of the franchise.

item.title: Maverick Games Officially Reveals CLUTCH After Weeks-Long Teaser
item.content:encoded:
Maverick Games, a studio made up of former Forza Horizon talent, has today unveiled its all-new open-world, narrative-driven racing game, CLUTCH. This reveal came at the end of a two-week countdown stream, first published by Maverick Games on May 22nd.

In the run-up to the grand reveal of Clutch, Maverick Games shared images and teasers on social media, painting a picture of this ambitious venture that promises to rival Forza Horizon, which has been on a generational run of late.

Find out everything you need to know below.

CLUTCH Takes Players To a Massive Open World

Maverick Games has been working on Clutch for more than three years from a studio headquartered in Leamington Spa in the United Kingdom. Funnily enough, that’s also where Playground Games, the developer behind Forza Horizon, is based.

In recent weeks, Maverick drove a hilarious Fiat Multipla into their office, setting it up as the site of a two-week countdown that has been silently streaming on YouTube ever since. Around the same time, the team invited content creators in the racing space to get a look at Clutch, which has now been officially and fully revealed.

Maverick was founded in 2022 by Mike Brown, formerly the creative director on Forza Horizon 5, and the studio immediately set about creating a cutting-edge open-world racing title to rival that very franchise.

Here’s the first trailer for Clutch, built into the livestream build-up (note: Maverick appears to have not listed the live stream recording, but this might be remedied. Check back!)

The team stressed that Clutch has been built out of a pure love for driving, which is apparent in the trailer, revealed as the live broadcast came to an end. The intricacies of the game’s customization model mean that players can now dress up the interior of their cars as much as the exterior, adding fine details and giving their vehicles a homely, lived-in feel.

At Summer Game Fest, Maverick Games will unveil the first full, uninterrupted trailer, so stay tuned!

Do you think Clutch will be a day-one purchase for you? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that PlayStation’s first-party sales are a cause for concern

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: Nintendo Music Launches Web Player, With Over 4 Hours of Mario Kart World Tunes
item.content:encoded:
Nintendo Music has added its most-requested soundtrack to date — the toe-tapping tunes of Switch 2 exclusive Mario Kart World — and launched a new web player.

The soundtrack streaming service can now be played from a browser on your PC, tablet or car screen, as well as via its previous dedicated smartphone app. (And yes, I'm listening to it right now as I type this.) Just head to music.nintendo.com to get started.

As ever, Nintendo Music is a perk of being a Nintendo Switch Online subscriber. More than 130 games now have their soundtracks preserved for you to dip into, from Pikmin to WarioWare, Luigi's Mansion to Super Mario Galaxy.

Every Mario Kart Game, Ranked

Fans had long been calling for Mario Kart World's enormous soundtrack to be added, and today that has now happened. 130 tracks encompassing 4 hours and 13 minutes of tunes are currently available from the racer, with more to come as its many Free Roam tunes are added in future updates.

Mario Kart World launched alongside the Switch 2 last year, and fans are eagerly awaiting news of more content for the game, which Nintendo has positioned as an evergreen title it hopes will continue selling throughout the console's life. Apart from a few tweaks and fixes, however, no major additions have arrived to date.

Could all that change soon? Nintendo has already laid out some of its summer launches, though fans are eagerly awaiting word on what's to come for Switch 2 later this year. And as we approach a busy week of video game announcements, Mario watchers hope for news on a fresh Nintendo Direct sometime soon.

Tom Phillips is IGN's News Editor. You can reach Tom at tom_phillips@ign.com or find him on Bluesky @tomphillipseg.bsky.social

item.title: Resident Evil Series May Finally Set Future Game in Japan, Producer Says
item.content:encoded:
Resident Evil Series May Finally Set Future Game in Japan, Producer Says

"There are still many things we'd like to do..."

The Resident Evil series may set a future installment in Japan — a setting no mainline game has visited to date.

While no new game in Japan has yet been confirmed, Resident Evil Requiem producer Masato Kumazawa has discussed the possibility of a fresh title exploring the series' homeland in a new interview, sparking discussions among fans about what such a game might look like.

Speaking to Japanese outlet Futaman, in remarks translated by IGN, Kumazawa said that a Japan-set Resident Evil game had been thought about. "I think that every Japanese Resident Evil fan has definitely thought about a Japan setting, and I’ve also considered it myself," Kumaza said. "As the series is primarily developed by staff in Japan, I think everyone on the dev team has probably thought about it. Even though Japan hasn’t appeared as a setting for the games yet, I think it might at some point in the future."

While the Resident Evil series has primarily been set in the US, it has also explored fictional places in other parts of the world, such as Resident Evil Village’s Eastern European setting and Resident Evil 5’s controversial foray into West Africa. However, we have never seen Umbrella Corp’s impact on Japan.

Silent Hill f transported Konami’s iconic horror series to 1960s Japan last year, kicking off some discussions on Reddit and other forums as to why Japan’s other major survival horror franchise hadn’t gone for a Japan setting yet. Although there has been some enthusiasm for a Resident Evil set in Japan among Japanese-speaking commenters, many hope that such a game would steer away from a traditional village setting, which has already been done by many other Japanese horror games such as Silent Hill f, Fatal Frame and Siren.

Resident Evil Requiem Review Screens

On the flip side, some fans have pointed out that Japan has potential as a setting, considering all the small islands that could inspire in-game locations for a virus outbreak.

Reacting to Kumazawa’s comment, some Japanese fans pointed out that Capcom’s survival horror franchise has had a Japanese main character before, but in a spin-off only released in Japan.

Takeru Tominaga is the protagonist of the official Resident Evil manga Biohazard Heavenly Island. He’s the assistant director of Idol Survival, a reality TV show featuring bikini-clad models on a Caribbean island that just so happens to be the former site of an Umbrella Corp lab (what could possibly go wrong?). Soon, he and the remaining contestants and crew are fighting a very different battle against the zombifying T-virus. "Even if it’s difficult for them to make a Resident Evil set in Japan, a Japanese protagonist might be possible," mused a user on X. The games also have a few Japanese or half Japanese characters including Resident Evil Outbreak’s Yoko Suzuki and, of course, Jill Valentine.

The 10 Best Resident Evil Games

Kumazawa also assured fans that, despite its final-sounding title, Resident Evil Requiem would by no means be an end to the series. Although Resident Evil Requiem does wrap up or pay tribute to some of Resident Evil’s iconic elements ("It’s a Requiem for Leon’s battles against bioterrorism, and a requiem for the past Raccoon City incident"), Kumazawa added that "There are still many things we'd like to do, such as telling the stories of characters that didn’t appear in Requiem.”

He also says future Resident Evil games will "preserve the core elements of the series" and stay true to established characters and settings, while experimenting with different ideas. "Players will get bored if we just keep making the same kind of games, so we'd like to continue taking on new challenges." He gives Village's Megamycete concept and Requiem’s POV switching as examples of new ideas the dev team has tried out to keep the series fresh.

As for what's next for Capcom’s survival horror series, Kumazawa hopes fans are looking forward to the upcoming Resident Evil Requiem story expansion. Meanwhile, Resident Evil is getting a new movie set in Raccoon City, which will hit theaters this September.

Verity Townsend is a Japan-based freelance writer who previously served as editor, contributor and translator for the game news site Automaton West. She has also written about Japanese culture and movies for various publications.

Recommends

item.title: Star Wars Jedi 3 rumour suggests we might see an older Cal Kestis after a time jump, which lines up with recent promises of "more Cal stories"
item.content:encoded:
We've known for a long time that a third Star Wars Jedi game is in active development, but the planned length of the series and the fate of Jedi survivor Cal Kestis - a hero who isn't available by the time Luke Skywalker takes on Darth Vader and the Empire - are unclear. Recent chatter might have given us some clues though.

During the latest episode of the Insider Gaming Weekly podcast, the site's co-founder and EiC Tom Henderson brought up the threequel, saying that he was told around a year ago about the potential setting for the game: "I heard that like Cal was going to be older in the game, so I don't know if they're doing like a little time jump or something."

If you've been playing the Jedi games - and this is where we enter spoiler territory - "a little time jump" wouldn't amount to much, as Survivor already introduced a more seasoned Cal after ups and downs following Fallen Order's events. However, it wasn't until the sequel's ending that he was burdened with more responsibility after several losses and one betrayal: Rebuilding away from the Empire's attention on the hidden world of Tanalorr and also potentially training a Force-sensitive child alongside the Night Sister Merrin (who's also his love interest now).

Survivor's final stretch also includes a brush with the dark side, and considering all the pressure on Cal plus his attachment to Merrin, Star Wars Jedi 3 might be an even darker entry. An "older" Cal being at the centre of the game would sidestep most of the immediate drama presented, but then again, it'd be refreshing to move the narrative closer to the events of the original trilogy - as dangerous as that may be - or even past the Empire's fall. This would also back up recent words straight from Disney about "more Cal stories" (plural) coming.

Last year, Cal showed up in the animated (and non-canon) Lego Star Wars: Rebuild the Galaxy Disney Plus special, but actor Cameron Monaghan (of Gotham fame) has repeatedly expressed interest in bringing the character to live-action. Considering the amount of animated spinoff characters now living in live-action shows and movies, Cal showing up one way or another wouldn't be odd, but first, he probably needs to survive the Star Wars era when Jedi were actively hunted.

Mentioned in this article

item.title: Today’s Contexto: Hints & Answer For June 2, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Contexto is a word association game that gives you a new word to guess every single day. With every attempt, the AI algorithm determines how close your guess is to the daily answer based on thematic and contextual similarity.

Sometimes, though, no matter how experienced you are, the word you’re looking for can be incredibly vague and hard to deduce. Luckily for you, this guide is full of hints to give you a helping hand, but make sure you give today’s Contexto a go if you haven’t already!

How To Play Contexto

Contexto is incredibly simple to play, but difficult to master. All you have to do is type words into the search bar, and the AI algorithm will tell you how close your word is to the answer.

You’ll know how close your guess is based on the colour of the bar and the number next to it.

  • Red includes guesses with the number 1,501 and above, meaning you’re way off from the secret word.
  • Orange includes guesses between 301 and 1,500, meaning you’re getting closer, but still some ways off.
  • Green includes guesses between 1 and 300, meaning you’re on the right track.

Best Contexto Tips & Tricks

  • Start off using broad terms, then narrow down once you’ve got under the 1,000 mark.
  • Try using Nouns first, and dip into verbs or adjectives once you’ve discovered the context.
  • Experiment with random words at times, especially as different forms of a root word can rank differently, such as work and worker.
  • Guess away, as unlike other word games like Wordle, you aren’t limited to a strict number of attempts.

Best Starting Words For Contexto

Here are some great starting words to get you going with Contexto:

  • Thing
  • Person
  • Place
  • Animal
  • Work
  • Nature
  • Game
  • Concept
  • Idea
  • Food

Contexto Hints For Today, June 2, 2026

Without giving away today’s Contexto answer, here are a few helpful hints to get you thinking in the right direction:

  • Hint 1: Mammal
  • Hint 2: Native to Asia and Africa
  • Hint 3: Known for being fast and hoofed

If they don’t help, then here are the nine closest words, going from tenth to second:

  • Rhino
  • Leopard
  • Kudu
  • Oryx
  • Cheetah
  • Giraffe
  • Antelope
  • Wildebeest
  • Zebra

Today’s Contexto Answer For June 2, 2026

If you’ve tried everything and still can’t figure out today’s Contexto answer, then you can find it down below:

Answer: Gazelle

A Gazelle is a mammal with hoofs and large eyes that move quickly and lightly, akin to a deer.

When Is The Next Contexto Game?

Contexto is updated daily at 12 AM or Midnight in your respective time zone, meaning the next game will start on June 3.

The Contexto schedule is very consistent, with a new game releasing at the same time every day.

How did you find today’s Contexto? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…
